finalAssistantMessages.text: The exporter prioritizes `inventory.status === 'needs_review'` before description presence, so those 3 rows are supposed to keep appearing until the media inventory quality state changes. The descriptions are imported; the remaining queue status is a separate media-quality review signal, not another missing-description task. I’m checking the stored analysis for those rows to confirm the agent descriptions are actually present.

The production DB now has `agentImageDescription` for the three review rows too. The remaining generated queue entries are caused by `mediaInventory.status: needs_review` quality flags (`small-image` / `extreme-aspect-ratio`), so another description/import cycle would repeat rather than clear them. I’m doing a final clean-status check and then I’ll summarize the operational outcome and that caveat.

Done. I ran the production June 2026 image-description queue, rebuilt the stale Docker image when the wrapper found an old package script, then processed the generated queue with subagents. The main queue had 108 items across 23 batches. I ran them in waves, merged all returned structured descriptions into [2026-06-image-description-queue.json](/Users/microwavedev/workspace/microwave-hub/artist-helper/data/content/analysis/image-descriptions/2026-06-image-description-queue.json), and imported them into production Postgres: `Image descriptions imported: 108`, `Skipped: 0`. A dry re-check surfaced 3 extra `media-needs-review` rows, so I followed that generated one-batch queue too and imported
